screenshot_note: Desktop 1280x800 and mobile 375x812 captured via Chromium headless. Office tab and Dashboard tab both screenshotted. Mobile confirms Bloberto speech bubble still overlaps the tab navigation bar (ESCALATED, 6th review). Dashboard tab on mobile shows stats cards in 2-col grid; 'ON THE CLOCK' label wraps to two lines on mobile. Luna card shows '15%' progress bar on desktop but workers.json has no progress field — bar rendering needs investigation. CSS vibe-reactive coffee corner labels confirmed ('COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT' on crushing).

suggestions.title: [ESCALATED #6] Speech bubble overflows office-floor and overlaps tab bar on mobile
suggestions.description: Office.css line 84: .office-floor { overflow: visible } is unchanged for the sixth consecutive review. The mobile screenshot (375x812) confirms Bloberto's speech bubble extends above the office-floor top border and visually covers the Office/Dashboard tab buttons, making them hard to click. The bubble is positioned via bottom: calc(100% + 8px) from inside a position:absolute parent with no clip boundary. Fix options: (1) Wrap .office-floor in a .office-floor-clip container with overflow: hidden, keep the floor itself at overflow: visible so desk tooltips still pop out. (2) Port the .speech-bubble to a React portal (ReactDOM.createPortal) outside the office floor entirely, positioned relative to viewport. Option 2 is more architecturally sound but larger. Option 1 is a 3-line CSS change. This has been escalated for six reviews with zero fix. Assigning to Maya as it is a CSS containment issue.

suggestions.title: NEW: stuckWorkers[0].emoji renders literal 'undefined' string in stuck stat card
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 511: the stuck stat card label is built as: `${stuckWorkers[0].emoji} ${stuckWorkers[0].name} · ...`. If the worker has no emoji field in workers.json (Carlos, Maya, Dave, Sofia all lack emoji in DEFAULT_ROSTER and the live JSON schema), JavaScript template literal coerces undefined to the string 'undefined', producing 'undefined Carlos · Working for 3h' in the UI. Fix: use optional chaining with a fallback: `${stuckWorkers[0].emoji ?? getRoleEmoji(stuckWorkers[0].role)} ${stuckWorkers[0].name} · ...`. getRoleEmoji is already defined at App.jsx line 159. This is a one-line fix that affects any user with a stuck non-Luna worker.

suggestions.title: NEW: ProgressBar with undefined progress renders '‍%' label and unpredictable bar width
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 221–237: the ProgressBar component renders `{progress}%` in the progress-pct span and sets the fill width via `Math.min(progress, 100)%`. When worker.progress is not present in workers.json (e.g., Luna in the live data currently), progress is undefined. `{undefined}%` in React JSX renders '%' with no number (confirmed in desktop dashboard screenshot — Luna's card shows 'PROGRESS ... %'). `Math.min(undefined, 100)` returns NaN; 'NaN%' is invalid CSS; the browser ignores the style override and the fill may render at 0 or full-width depending on the CSS cascade. Fix: add a guard in ProgressBar — if progress is null or undefined, render nothing or a neutral empty bar: `if (progress == null || isNaN(progress)) return null`. Also guard in the WorkerCard render site: only render ProgressBar when typeof worker.progress === 'number'. The App.jsx call on line 346 passes worker.progress without checking.

suggestions.title: NEW: Keyboard arrow tab navigation does not update window.location.hash
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 739–748: handleTabKeyDown handles ArrowLeft/ArrowRight/ArrowUp/ArrowDown to switch tabs, calling setTab() but NOT updating window.location.hash. The click handlers at lines 1184 and 1197 both call window.location.hash = tabName, and handleWorkerClick at line 752 also updates hash on navigation. The keyboard path is inconsistent — a user who uses arrow keys to switch to Dashboard will find the browser URL still shows #office, and pressing browser Back will not undo the tab switch. Fix: add window.location.hash = TABS[(currentIdx + 1) % TABS.length] inside both branches of handleTabKeyDown, matching the click handler pattern.

suggestions.title: [Carry-over] Paused pill says 'move mouse to refresh' but refresh takes up to 30 seconds
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1148: the paused pill reads '⏸ Paused · move mouse to refresh'. Moving the mouse calls the touch handler which sets pollingPaused(false) but does NOT immediately call syncFromGitHub(). The next fetch only happens on the next pollTick interval (30s). A user who moves the mouse expecting immediate fresh data will wait up to 30 seconds with no visible feedback. Two fixes: (1) Change text to '⏸ Paused · move mouse to resume' (copy fix, small effort). (2) Inside the touch handler, if pollingPaused was true before the mouse move, call syncFromGitHub() immediately (functional fix, also small). The click on the paused stat card (line 1211 onResume) also only sets state — same issue there.

suggestions.title: NEW: activityEntries filter+slice in JSX defeats React.memo on WorkerCard
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1232, 1255, 1268: each WorkerCard receives activityEntries={activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === w.name).slice(-3)}. This creates a new array reference on every render of the parent App component. WorkerCard is wrapped in React.memo (line 269), but React.memo uses referential equality by default — a new array reference on every render means WorkerCard ALWAYS re-renders regardless of whether the data changed. The same pattern is used in all three WorkerCard render sites. Fix: compute per-worker activity slices in a useMemo keyed on [activityLog]: const activityByWorker = useMemo(() => Object.fromEntries(roster.map(r => [r.name, activityLog.filter(e => e.worker === r.name).slice(-3)])), [activityLog, roster]); then pass activityByWorker[w.name] ?? [] at each call site. This makes React.memo actually effective.

suggestions.title: [Carry-over] Activity filter logic duplicated in two places
suggestions.description: App.jsx: the identical type+worker filter chain appears at lines 1282–1294 (to compute a count for the section header) and at lines 1335–1344 (to compute the entries passed to ActivityLog). Changing a filter condition requires editing both. Extract to a single useMemo: const filteredEntries = useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(typeFilter).filter(workerFilter), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter]); use filteredEntries.length for the count and filteredEntries for the ActivityLog prop.

suggestions.title: [Carry-over] tabIndex={0} on ALL Character elements including non-interactive ghosts
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 682: tabIndex={0} is set unconditionally on every Character div, including ghost roster members that have no onClick handler (onClick is undefined). Keyboard users tabbing through the office floor will land on 6+ ghost characters that do nothing when activated — an accessibility dead end. Fix: set tabIndex conditionally: tabIndex={handleClick ? 0 : -1}. The role={handleClick ? 'button' : undefined} check at line 681 already correctly omits the button role for non-interactive chars; tabIndex should follow the same pattern.

suggestions.title: NEW: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS, WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES, ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES defined inside Character component body
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 510, 526–533: three constant lookup objects (IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS, ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES, WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES) are defined inside the Character component function body. They are re-created as new object literals on every render of every Character instance. Since there can be up to 6+ Character instances and the ambient interval fires every 8–45 seconds triggering re-renders, these allocations accumulate unnecessarily. Fix: move all three to module-level constants (outside the component, alongside the existing PING_REACTIONS, BLOBERTO_TITLES, etc.). Zero behavioral change, marginally better performance, and makes the code easier to scan.

suggestions.title: [Carry-over] Pingable worker Characters lack aria-label describing the ping action
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1453 and 1481: Characters rendered with onClick get role='button' but no aria-label. A screen reader will read only the character's visible name ('Luna', 'Carlos', etc.) with no indication of the interactive affordance. Fix: add aria-label={'Ping ' + worker.name} (or 'Click to inspect ' + worker.name) to the Character component when onClick is provided. A one-line change at the Character call site or inside the component itself.

suggestions.title: NEW: CSS ::after vibe labels on coffee corner are invisible to screen readers
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 1662–1667: vibe-reactive text is appended to .coffee-corner__label via CSS content, e.g., [data-vibe='crushing'] .coffee-corner__label::after { content: ' TRIPLE SHOT'; } and [data-vibe='on-fire'] .coffee-corner__label::after { content: ' STRONG. NOW.'; }. CSS-generated content via ::after pseudo-elements is typically not announced by screen readers (it is in the accessibility tree on some browsers but not consistently). The sr-only span inside the coffee corner (Office.jsx line 1427) only reads 'Coffee corner' with no vibe context. Low priority since it is decorative, but if the vibe label is informational, expose it via the sr-only span instead: e.g., Coffee corner — {vibe === 'crushing' ? 'triple shot' : '...'}.

suggestions.title: NEW: isAfterHours (time-based, hours<5) diverges from teamVibe 'after-hours' (worker-count-based) in header
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1056: const isAfterHours = currentHour < 5. This flags midnight–4am as 'after hours'. But getTeamVibe() returns 'after-hours' only when workers.length === 0 (vibe.js line 7). These two concepts are independent and can conflict. If it is 3am and the team has active workers, isAfterHours is true but teamVibe.key is 'crushing'. App.jsx line 1128 uses: HONORIFICS[isAfterHours ? 'after-hours' : teamVibe.key] — so at 3am with a crushing team, the greeting says 'night owl 🌙' rather than 'LEGEND 🔥'. Fix: remove the isAfterHours override in the HONORIFICS lookup and just use teamVibe.key. The greeting text already handles the after-hours case contextually. Keep isAfterHours only for the greeting prefix (line 1058) where time-of-day is the right signal.

suggestions.title: [Carry-over] IIFE inside JSX for solo spotlight message
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1239: solo spotlight message computed via an IIFE inside JSX. Extract to a pre-return variable: const soloKey = (activeWorkers[0]?.id ?? activeWorkers[0]?.name)?.toLowerCase(); const soloMsg = SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES[soloKey]?.[teamVibe.key] ?? SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES[soloKey]?.default ?? '🌟 Running solo today...'; then use soloMsg in JSX. Carry-over from review 42.

suggestions.title: [Carry-over] stuckWorkers useMemo captures Date.now() without declaring it as dependency
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 438–441: useMemo(() => workers.filter(w => ... Date.now() ...), [workers]). Date.now() is called at memo-evaluation time but is not a declared dependency. If a worker crosses the 2-hour threshold between worker fetches, the stuck badge won't appear until the next workers state update. The memo is cheap enough to compute as a plain derived variable outside useMemo entirely. Carry-over from review 42.

suggestions.title: [Carry-over] plantStageNames array declared but never used
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1066: const plantStageNames = ['seedling', 'sprout', 'small-plant', 'leafy', 'blooming'] is declared and never referenced. The stage is computed via ternary chain on lines 1067–1071. Delete the array. Carry-over from review 42.

suggestions.title: [Carry-over] Duplicate vibe CSS selectors — single-quote and double-quote versions coexist
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91–95 define .office-floor[data-vibe='crushing'] etc. with single-quoted attribute selectors (border-color + box-shadow only). Lines 97–115 redefine the same selectors with double-quoted values AND set --grid-line. Since single and double quotes are equivalent in CSS attribute selectors, the earlier single-quote rules for crushing/on-fire are redundant (same specificity, overridden by the later double-quote rules). Delete lines 91–95. The in-flow, slow-day, and after-hours rules also have equivalent single/double copies. Carry-over from review 42.
